factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices in certain segments.

evidence.text: DTS and SCS operating revenue growth was due to new business, increased volumes and higher pricing in the second quarter and first half of 2016.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company successfully raised prices, contributing to revenue growth.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is stable with consistent performance across segments.

evidence.text: Total revenue increased 2% to $1.70 billion and operating revenue increased 4% to $1.45 billion in the second quarter of 2016. For the first half of 2016, total revenue increased 3% to $3.33 billion and operating revenue increased 6% to $2.86 billion.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth is stable with consistent performance across segments.

factors.summary: End market momentum varies with growth in DTS and SCS segments offsetting declines in others.

evidence.text: DTS and SCS operating revenue growth was due to new business, increased volumes and higher pricing in the second quarter and first half of 2016. However, commercial rental demand declined, reflecting lower demand.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed performance with growth in some segments offsetting declines in others.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, particularly due to pension expenses, creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: SG&A expenses increased 4% in the second quarter of 2016 compared with the year-earlier period and increased 3% in the first half of 2016. The increase... is primarily due to increased pension expenses, partially offset by lower professional fees, compensation-related expenses and foreign exchange.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing, particularly due to pension expenses, creating margin pressure.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign exchange and fuel prices.

evidence.text: Foreign exchange negatively impacted revenue growth by 100 basis points. Lower fuel prices passed through to customers also impacted revenue growth.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Key sensitivities include foreign exchange and fuel prices, which negatively impact revenue.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange is impacting margins negatively.

evidence.text: Foreign exchange negatively impacted revenue growth by 100 basis points. Foreign exchange also reduced cost of services by 100 basis points.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange is negatively impacting margins.

factors.summary: Management provides forward-looking guidance on expectations for operating cash flow, capital expenditures, and fuel price impacts.

evidence.text: •our expectations of operating cash flow and capital expenditures through the end of 2016; •the anticipated impact of fuel price fluctuations;
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Management provides forward-looking guidance on expectations without clear positive or negative signals.

factors.summary: Management demonstrates confidence through maintaining credit facilities, declaring dividends, and focusing on non-GAAP measures.

evidence.text: We maintain a global revolving credit facility used to finance working capital. The availability under our global revolving credit facility is $1.2 billion and the facility matures in January 2020. In May 2016, our Board of Directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.41 per share of common stock.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management demonstrates confidence through maintaining credit facilities and declaring dividends.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are a headwind, with risks of decreased demand, lower profit margins, and reduced access to credit.

evidence.text: •Market Conditions: Changes in general economic and financial conditions in the U.S. and worldwide leading to decreased demand for our services, lower profit margins, increased levels of bad debt and reduced access to credit; •Decreases in freight demand which would impact both our transactional and variable-based contractual business.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates economic conditions are a headwind with risks of decreased demand and lower profit margins.

factors.summary: Volume trends are mixed, with growth in some areas such as services revenue, but declines in others due to lower fuel prices and market demand. Additionally, unit volumes or transaction counts are expected to decline further due to decreases in market demand, used vehicle sales, and global economic conditions.

evidence.text: Services revenue increased 7% in the second quarter and 8% in the first half of 2016 due to new business, increased volumes and higher pricing in the SCS and DTS segments. However, fuel services revenue decreased 19% in the second quarter of 2016 and 24% in the first half of 2016 due to lower fuel prices passed through to customers.
•Further decreases in market demand affecting the commercial rental market and used vehicle sales as well as global economic conditions.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Volume trends show mixed performance with declines in certain areas due to lower fuel prices and market demand.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, with some growth in certain segments but lower demand in others. While the company expects growth in its FMS business segment, particularly in full service lease and commercial rental revenue, other segments like DTS and SCS anticipate operating revenue growth. However, there is evidence of lower demand in commercial rental revenue, reflecting broader market dynamics.

evidence.text: Lease and rental revenues increased 2% in the second quarter of 2016 and 4% in the first half of 2016 primarily driven by a 5% larger average full service lease fleet and higher prices on replacement vehicles, partially offset by lower commercial rental revenue reflecting lower demand.

•our expectations in our FMS business segment regarding anticipated full service lease and commercial rental revenue and demand; •our expectations in our DTS and SCS business segments regarding anticipated operating revenue trends and growth rates;
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Demand trends are mixed with some growth in certain segments but lower demand in others, reflecting broader market dynamics.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel, are a significant factor impacting profitability and customer pricing. While declining fuel prices have provided some benefit by reducing costs and being passed through to customers, fuel remains a major cost driver that can impact financial performance.

evidence.text: Fuel services revenue decreased 19% in the second quarter of 2016 and 24% in the first half of 2016 due to lower fuel prices passed through to customers. Cost of fuel services decreased 19% in the second quarter and 23% in the first half of 2016 as a result of lower fuel prices.

Fuel : We exclude FMS, DTS and SCS fuel from the calculation of our operating revenue measures, as fuel is an ancillary service that we provide our customers, which is impacted by fluctuations in market fuel prices, and the costs are largely a pass-through to our customers, resulting in minimal changes in our profitability during periods of steady market fuel prices.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Input costs, particularly fuel, are a significant factor but the evidence shows both positive and negative impacts on profitability.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory shows mixed performance, with compression in gross and operating margins due to factors like foreign exchange and lower fuel prices, while management highlights efforts to maintain margins through non-GAAP financial measures and comparable earnings.

evidence.text: Lease and rental gross margin decreased 2% in the second quarter and remained consistent with the first half of 2015. Services gross margin increased 4% in the second quarter and 9% in the first half of 2016. However, EBT decreased 12% in the second quarter of 2016 and 6% in the first half of 2016, reflecting lower commercial rental and used vehicle results, partially offset by higher full service lease results, lower insurance costs in DTS and increased pricing, new business and increased volumes in DTS and SCS.

Comparable earnings, comparable earnings per diluted common share (EPS) and comparable earnings before income tax are defined, respectively, as GAAP earnings, EPS and earnings before income tax, all from continuing operations, excluding (1) non-operating pension costs and operations (2) any other significant items that are not representative of our business.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The margin trajectory shows mixed performance with some compression in margins but efforts to maintain through non-GAAP measures.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include foreign exchange impacts, lower fuel prices, economic conditions, fuel price volatility, decreased demand, and freight demand fluctuations. There is some tension in the evidence, as lower fuel prices are noted to have impacted revenue growth, while fuel price volatility and shortages are also highlighted as risks.

evidence.text: Foreign exchange negatively impacted revenue growth by 100 basis points. Lower fuel prices passed through to customers also impacted revenue growth.

•Market Conditions: Changes in general economic and financial conditions in the U.S. and worldwide leading to decreased demand for our services, lower profit margins, increased levels of bad debt and reduced access to credit; •Sudden changes in fuel prices and fuel shortages; •Decreases in freight demand which would impact both our transactional and variable-based contractual business.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include foreign exchange impacts, lower fuel prices, and economic conditions, which are noted as significant risks.
